content.full_text:
March 13, 1990

Ms. Jill Stecher  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street, Suite 3800  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, CA 95804-0814

Dear Ms. Stecher:

I spoke to you earlier this year regarding a conflicts question concerning the Davis City Council election. I am an attorney in this law firm and a candidate for the Davis City Council, which election is June 5, 1990. The question I posed to you on the telephone was whether it is a conflict for me to sit on the same city council as a partner of my law firm, David Rosenberg. As I informed you, individuals have questioned whether a conflict of interest exists for employer/employee to sit on the same city council. You informed me that it was your opinion that the situation does not raise a potential conflict of interest based on relevant sections of the California Elections Code which deal primarily with conflicts based on financial interest, as in the case where a client would appear before the city council.

March 13, 1990

Because this conflicts question continues to be raised, I am requesting a formal opinion letter regarding this conflicts question:

Does a conflict of interest exist for an associate attorney to sit on the same city council as a partner of the same law firm?

Thank you for your time and consideration with this question. I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Very truly yours,

DIEPENBROCK, WULFF, PLANT & HANNEGAN

By Melinda Guzman Moore
